questions: You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. A coworker used your favorite mug from the kitchen and put it back dirty. How do you respond?
You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. Your friend posted a group photo on social media but cropped you out. What do you do?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. A neighbor returned the tool they borrowed, but with a small scratch on it. How do you handle it?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. Your sibling got a slightly bigger piece of cake at the family dinner. What goes through your mind?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. A colleague didn't CC you on an email about a project you're tangentially involved in. What do you do?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. Someone at work parked in the spot you always use, even though spots aren't assigned. How do you react?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. Your friend invited everyone from the group to their party except you, then said it was an oversight. What do you think?
You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. A restaurant server gave your table's bread basket to the next table. How do you respond?
You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. Your partner forgot to thank you for picking up groceries on the way home. What do you say?

You're someone who focuses on what matters and easily lets go of small slights and trivial annoyances. A classmate borrowed your notes and returned them with a coffee stain on the first page. How do you handle it?
